title: Sell in May? We’d Rather Buy in July With 2 Tickers on Our List
author: Brett Owens
description: The article argues July is the strongest month for stocks historically, with the S&P 500 rising 2.3% on average over 20 years. The author recommends buying two dividend-growth stocks: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) and Deere (DE). ITW is highlighted for its high operating margins, consistent dividend growth, and share buybacks despite analyst neglect. Deere is positioned to benefit from a construction boom while the agricultural segment is at a cycle bottom, with corn and wheat prices showing signs of recovery.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrates strong fundamentals with 5% revenue growth, 12% EPS gains, raised guidance, high operating margins (25.4%), nearly tripled dividend over a decade, and 17% share buybacks. Analyst neglect creates upside potential as expectations are beaten.

Positioned in a 'sweet spot' with booming construction-equipment segment forecasting 20% sales gains and 10-12% margin expansion in 2026. Agricultural segment at cycle bottom with corn/wheat prices firming. Low payout ratio (47%) supports future dividend growth. Benefits from expected fuel cost declines and infrastructure spending.

title: Husker Harvest Days 2026 registration opens with new equipment demonstrations
author: Na
description: Husker Harvest Days 2026, the Western Corn Belt's premier agricultural showcase, opens registration for its Sept. 15-17 event in Grand Island, Nebraska. The event features new live demonstrations of corn stalk baling, tub grinders, and feed mixers, alongside returning attractions like the HHD Drone Zone and 12 field demonstration categories. Free admission is available for pre-registered attendees.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: John Deere is mentioned only as a sponsor of an unrelated award (Organic Grower Summit's 2026 Grower of the Year Award) in promotional content at the end of the article, with no direct connection to the main Husker Harvest Days event or its activities.

title: Farm Progress Show 2026 unveils major innovations
author: Not Specified
description: The Farm Progress Show returns to Boone, Iowa, Sept. 1-3, 2026, with three major additions: an expanded Agronomy Zone featuring research-grade crop plots with the Hefty brothers from Ag PhD, new premium ticket packages at $60, and a Drone Zone for precision agriculture demonstrations. The event previously attracted 161,000 attendees and 548 exhibitors in 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: John Deere is mentioned only as a sponsor of the Organic Grower Summit's 2026 Grower of the Year Award in promotional content at the end, with no substantive involvement in the main Farm Progress Show announcements.

title: Nominations Open for Organic Grower Summit's 2026 Grower of the Year Award
author: Farm Progress
description: The Organic Grower Summit, presented by Farm Progress and sponsored by John Deere, is accepting nominations for its ninth annual Grower of the Year Award. The award will recognize four finalists who demonstrate innovation, sustainability, and leadership in organic agriculture. The summit will take place December 2-3, 2026, in Monterey, California, with the winner announced during the keynote presentation. Nominations close August 31, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: John Deere's sponsorship of the Grower of the Year Award demonstrates active engagement with the organic agriculture sector and positions the company as a supporter of sustainable farming innovation and industry leadership.

title: United Rentals: The Industrial Stock Workhorse I'd Happily Hold Through Any Market Crash
author: Todd Shriber
description: United Rentals has delivered exceptional returns over the past decade with a 1,360% gain, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. The equipment rental company benefits from strong fundamentals including 10% revenue CAGR and 20% EPS CAGR, market leadership with 16% share of North American equipment rental market, and exposure to growing data center and utilities sectors. The company is also returning capital to shareholders through a $5 billion buyback program and 10% dividend increase, though valuation concerns and debt levels present some risks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong historical performance (1,360% return over 10 years), solid growth metrics (10% revenue CAGR, 20% EPS CAGR), market leadership position with 16% market share, exposure to high-growth sectors (data centers, utilities), and commitment to shareholder returns through buybacks and dividend increases. While valuation concerns and debt levels are noted as risks, the overall assessment is favorable for long-term investors.
Mentioned as a manufacturer of equipment that United Rentals leases, but no specific analysis or sentiment is provided about the company itself.
